notes: This bundle is a proxy comparison for P1-E01. The repository does not contain a GPU, a live Triton instance, or a populated detector evaluation set, so the full protocol cannot be executed in this environment.

Composite scores follow docs/bake-off-protocol.md. Throughput is evaluated against the pilot target of 40 FPS aggregate, so every candidate saturates the throughput term on the target 24 GB GPU class.
night_ap is neutralized to the mean overall mAP across the three candidates because no primary-source night-slice benchmark was found for YOLOv8-L, YOLOv9-C, or RT-DETR-L.
YOLOv8-L small_object_ap is imputed as the neutral median of the published YOLOv9-C and RT-DETR-L AP_small values because no primary-source COCO AP_small slice was found for YOLOv8-L.
mAP and params come from the official Ultralytics YOLOv8 model card.
The 24 GB target-GPU throughput band comes directly from docs/triton-placement.md.
Because the throughput term caps at 1.0 above 40 FPS, YOLOv8-L's score is driven by accuracy and the neutralized slice terms rather than speed.
mAP and AP_small come from the official YOLOv9 README / repo evaluation block.

The 24 GB target-GPU estimate is inferred by scaling the repo's YOLOv8-L baseline from docs/triton-placement.md by the official YOLOv9 issue benchmark ratio on Tesla T4 (154.20 FPS vs 136.71 FPS for YOLOv8-L).

YOLOv9-C has the best published small-object slice among the three candidates, but the advantage is not large enough to create a 0.02 composite-score lead after the protocol's safe-default rule is applied.
mAP and T4 FPS come from the official RT-DETR model card / README.
AP_small comes from the official RT-DETR COCO training log attachment for the HGNetv2-L checkpoint that matches the published 53.0 AP / 114 FPS profile.
RT-DETR-L likely has the strongest global-context story for unusual or crowded scenes, but published benchmark data still leaves it marginally behind on the proxy score.
